texto: Whose boys are those with the holes in their pants?"I cringed. How would my grandpa answer such an embarrassing question?"Those are my daughter's children, my grandchildren," Grandpa spoke as if he'd just been asked who had won first place in a foot race or just been baptized into the church."Those pants are very well ironed," the stranger said.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elizabeth Byler Younts: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Whose boys are those with the holes in their pants?"I cringed. How would my grandpa answer such a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, poverty, those pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
